channel.title: yourfdc.au News — Family Day Care Australia
channel.description: Australia's dedicated Family Day Care news hub.

item.title: Expanding childcare supply starts with asking the right questions
item.description: Australia's early childhood education and care sector finds itself at a pivotal moment.
item.content:encoded: Australia's early childhood education and care sector finds itself at a pivotal moment.
item.dc:creator: The Sector

item.title: Most popular NSW childcare suburbs and where it’s easy to get a spot - Herald Sun
item.description: Most popular NSW childcare suburbs and where it’s easy to get a spot - Herald Sun
item.content:encoded: Most popular NSW childcare suburbs and where it’s easy to get a spot - Herald Sun
item.dc:creator: Herald Sun

item.title: What Australia can learn from the UK's "Experts at Hand" approach
item.description: Australia's early childhood, disability and education systems are undergoing significant change.
item.content:encoded: Australia's early childhood, disability and education systems are undergoing significant change.
item.dc:creator: The Sector

item.title: Why human oversight must remain central to technology in early childhood education
item.description: Australian early childhood education and care has entered a challenging period of heightened scrutiny, reform and public expectation. Across the sector, there is growing emphasis on child-safe governance, stronger accountability, clearer evidence, and better systems for identifying and responding to risk.
item.content:encoded: Australian early childhood education and care has entered a challenging period of heightened scrutiny, reform and public expectation. Across the sector, there is growing emphasis on child-safe governance, stronger accountability, clearer evidence, and better systems for identifying and responding to risk.
item.dc:creator: The Sector

item.title: Red Nose opens Sydney hub to strengthen bereavement support for families
item.description: Red Nose Australia has opened a new Sydney Hub in Gladesville, creating a dedicated space for families grieving the loss of a baby or child to access face-to-face counselling, peer support and community-based bereavement programs.
item.content:encoded: Red Nose Australia has opened a new Sydney Hub in Gladesville, creating a dedicated space for families grieving the loss of a baby or child to access face-to-face counselling, peer support and community-based bereavement programs.
item.dc:creator: The Sector

item.title: Moving beyond symbols to embodied understanding: reimagining reconciliation in the outside school hours care space
item.description: Across Australia, early childhood and outside school hours care services are being challenged to move beyond symbolic acknowledgements of First Nations cultures and towards genuine reconciliation. While flags, artwork, and cultural celebrations remain important, many educators are asking how we can support children to develop a deeper understanding of reconciliation as a lived practice rather than a symbolic concept.
item.content:encoded: Across Australia, early childhood and outside school hours care services are being challenged to move beyond symbolic acknowledgements of First Nations cultures and towards genuine reconciliation. While flags, artwork, and cultural celebrations remain important, many educators are asking how we can support children to develop a deeper understanding of reconciliation as a lived practice rather than a symbolic concept.
item.dc:creator: The Sector

item.title: Workforce sustainability emerges as defining challenge at The Sector Workforce and Wellbeing Forum
item.description: The release of the Child Safety Commission of Inquiry’s landmark report, From Pressure to Purpose, is a moment that demands more than a polite nod from the Early Childhood Education and Care sector.
item.content:encoded: The release of the Child Safety Commission of Inquiry’s landmark report, From Pressure to Purpose, is a moment that demands more than a polite nod from the Early Childhood Education and Care sector.
item.dc:creator: The Sector

item.title: Queensland approved provider fined $15,000 after toddler leaves bush excursion unnoticed
item.description: A Queensland approved provider has been fined $15,000 after a 23-month-old child left a bush excursion unnoticed and remained unsupervised for up to 23 minutes, in an incident that has reinforced the importance of supervision and robust risk assessment practices in early childhood education and care.
item.content:encoded: A Queensland approved provider has been fined $15,000 after a 23-month-old child left a bush excursion unnoticed and remained unsupervised for up to 23 minutes, in an incident that has reinforced the importance of supervision and robust risk assessment practices in early childhood education and care.
item.dc:creator: The Sector

item.title: Report shows how young people want to use their voice for change
item.description: Young Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander people want authentic representation, decision-making power and community-led structures that deliver real outcomes, according to a landmark national report released today.
item.content:encoded: Young Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander people want authentic representation, decision-making power and community-led structures that deliver real outcomes, according to a landmark national report released today.
item.dc:creator: SNAICC

item.title: Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander peoples ready to work in partnership for a safer future for Queensland children
item.description: SNAICC – National Voice for our Children CEO Catherine Liddle has urged the Queensland Government to work in genuine partnership with Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander people and communities in reforming the State’s child protection system.
item.content:encoded: SNAICC – National Voice for our Children CEO Catherine Liddle has urged the Queensland Government to work in genuine partnership with Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander people and communities in reforming the State’s child protection system.
item.dc:creator: SNAICC

item.title: SNAICC in the News | 5 June 2026
item.description: In this week’s SNAICC in the News, SNAICC responds to proposed NT child protection laws and the Children’s Commissioner’s report, and announces Marlee Silva as 2026 Children’s Day Ambassador.

item.title: From Country to Classroom: SNAICC Launches Culturally Grounded Learning Tools
item.description: SNAICC – National Voice for our Children has launched a unique culturally informed early Childhood Education and Care resource for early childhood workers. The Bushland Animal Yoga Cards’ offer ACCO and non-Indigenous ECEC services a resource that incorporates movement with Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander languages, with native Australian animals representing various poses and movements.
item.content:encoded: SNAICC – National Voice for our Children has launched a unique culturally informed early Childhood Education and Care resource for early childhood workers. The Bushland Animal Yoga Cards’ offer ACCO and non-Indigenous ECEC services a resource that incorporates movement with Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander languages, with native Australian animals representing various poses and movements.
item.dc:creator: SNAICC

item.title: To improve children’s mental health, start by supporting their parents
item.description: Long-term data shows children are more likely to develop mental health issues when their parents experience financial stress and housing instability.

item.dc:creator: The Conversation
